what_input_feature: An XML attribute whose split name resolves to the exact byte sequence name[0]='x', name[1]='m', name[2]='l', name[3]='n', name[4]='s', name[5]=0 (the literal 'xmlns' with no prefix and ns==NULL), satisfying the full six-character equality chain at SAX2.c:1170-1171.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'xmlns=' token in their bytes (the ASCII run 'xmlns' is the program-side target of the gate) and value_profile_cmplog can splice the exact 5-char/NUL-terminated comparand into the right input offset via I2SRandReplace; the winner reaches the false/resolving side at line 1160 and 1333 ([W] tags) and resolves 8/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile only has havoc plus a CMP_MAP distance gradient; for a 6-way single-byte equality chain that terminates on a NUL, the prefix-distance bucket gives no useful gradient past the matched prefix, so the loser blocks 8/10 trials and only reaches the gate's true side once.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: cmplog records the per-character comparand bytes ('x','m','l','n','s',0) at the SAX2.c:1170-1171 CMPs and substitutes them into the matching input offset, planting the exact 'xmlns\0' name that value_profile's Hamming/prefix gradient alone cannot synthesize.

what_input_feature: A byte at the parse cursor with the high bit set (c & 0x80) forming a multibyte UTF-8 sequence whose decoded value val fails IS_CHAR(val), e.g. a 0xf5/0xff lead-byte run that produces an out-of-allowed-range codepoint.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins with f5 ff ff ff ff ff ff ff: 0xf5 and 0xff have the high bit set, so c & 0x80 is true and the bytes are decoded as a multibyte sequence whose resulting val lands out of the IS_CHAR range, taking the blocked TRUE side and reaching the xmlErrEncodingInt call at lines 765-767.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seed reaches line 764 but only on the False side (T=0 F=3): its decoded multibyte values stay inside the allowed XML char range, so IS_CHAR(val) is true and the TRUE branch is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that value_profile lacks; I2SRandReplace splices logged comparand bytes into the input at the cursor offset, constructing the exact high-bit multibyte lead sequence that decodes to an out-of-range val, which value_profile's distance-feedback-only search fails to assemble within budget.

what_input_feature: Input bytes must form a syntactically valid start tag that declares at least one namespace (xmlns/xmlns:prefix) AND closes as an empty element with the literal '/>' so xmlParseStartTag2 increments ctxt->nsNr above the saved nsNr and the empty-element close path at line 11687 evaluates ctxt->nsNr - nsNr > 0 as true.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed carries a coherent XML-declaration plus element prologue ('1.0"?>.<!', 'aml.version="1.0"?>.<!DOCTY') aligning the parser into START_TAG handling; the winner reaches line 11688 nsPop (marked [W]) and the '&' reference path (11765-11768 [W]), evidence it parses element content with namespaces and pops them on the empty-element close.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds stall in DTD/declaration text ('EMENT b (CDATA)>', '<!ATTLIST b xmlns:xlin', '#FIXED', 'http://www...') and never construct a closed namespaced empty element; coverage shows the loser takes the false branch at 11687 (T=0 F=58 at L11687) and never reaches the nsPop at 11688, so nsNr - nsNr is 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: it splices logged CMP operands (the per-byte tag/namespace literal comparands inside xmlParseStartTag2/xmlParseQName) directly into the input bytes, assembling the exact 'xmlns'-bearing start tag and '/>' close that value_profile's gradient feedback plus havoc could not synthesize, letting the winner cross the multi-byte structural gate.
